__fixture__.name: settings row: supported + stable + live
__fixture__.scenario: A settings surface shows a strong support claim with lifecycle, channel, freshness, client scope, deployment scope, and certified-archetype/compatibility cues. Overflow preserves hidden badge families and exported text matches UI labels.
contract_sections: Allowed combinations and denial / downgrade rules

support_class.explanation_sentence: This support claim is scoped to the disclosed clients and deployment cues and is backed by current evidence for the shown compatibility envelope.

lifecycle.explanation_sentence: The capability is in a stable lifecycle posture; deprecations or policy disablement must surface separately and never be implied by color alone.

channel.explanation_sentence: This build carries the capability on the stable release channel for the declared scope.

freshness.explanation_sentence: Evidence backing this badge group is within its freshness window and reflects current policy posture.

deployment_scope.explanation_sentence: This claim is about local execution on the workstation; it does not imply managed or hosted availability.

client_scope.explanation_sentence: The capability is supported on the disclosed client surfaces; other clients must not assume parity.

certified_archetype_state.explanation_sentence: A current compatibility or certified-archetype row backs the claim for the declared tuple; if the backing row becomes stale, this badge must narrow to a retest-required posture.

compatibility_state.explanation_sentence: The current environment tuple matches the supported envelope; compatibility narrowing must remain explicit when it changes.

invalid_combinations.summary: Strong support claim without freshness disclosure
invalid_combinations.reason: A strong support claim must carry a freshness badge so exported text and screenshots do not imply evergreen proof.
A mismatch must refuse strong support claims rather than leaving certified/supported text visible.

invalid_combinations.summary: Badge meaning conveyed only by hue
invalid_combinations.reason: Badges must remain readable in high-contrast, forced-colors, screenshots, and export; label text is mandatory.

expected_projection.export_text: Supported · Stable · Live · 4 more (Channel, Client scope, Deployment scope, Compatibility)
